name: Trigger Agent
description: Fallback kick that nudges the agent's run loop to execute a cycle. Not a result-getter — trigger carries no payload and makes no guarantee about what the agent produces. For results, await the relevant Job: submit work via agent:request (task Job) or agent:chat (chat Job) and wait on that. Use agent:trigger only when something outside the normal intake path needs a nudge — e.g. after a manual state edit, for diagnostics, or to resume a stuck agent. Creates no session. Defaults to blocking until the current cycle yields or finishes; set wait=false to return immediately, or wait=<ms> for a bounded wait.

force.description: true (default): run a cycle even if the agent has no outstanding work. false: run only if there is work — a no-op on an idle agent. The venue scheduler fires deferred wakes with force=false.

wait.description: Wait policy: true (default, block until cycle yields or completes), false (return immediately), or integer milliseconds (timeout). Not a result-await — wait on the task/chat Job for results. Cancelling this trigger Job ends the wait early; the run loop is unaffected and continues for any other waiters.

sessionId.description: Optional session identifier (hex string). If supplied it is resolved and echoed back; trigger never creates a session. If omitted the trigger runs unsessioned.
Echoed only if a sessionId was supplied in the request

status.description: Agent status at return: SLEEPING (cycle quiesced) or RUNNING (cycle still in progress, yielded on async op, or wait=false / timed out)

result.description: Transition result snapshot if the cycle completed during the wait. Do NOT use this to read agent output — await the task/chat Job instead.
